# Google Calendar Module LLM Guide

Purpose: two-way-ready Google Calendar sync for Cloudflare Workers + D1. Connect a
calendar, keep its OAuth token fresh, keep push channels alive, sync events
incrementally, and expand recurrences for display.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: connectCalendar,
  refreshAccessToken, syncCalendar, renewExpiringChannels, handlePushNotification,
  listEvents (plus the shared applyEvents dedup helper).
- `src/rrule.ts` is the PURE recurrence expander (parseRRule, expandRecurrence).
- `src/ports/` defines GoogleCalendarClient, TokenStore, SyncStateStore,
  ChannelStore, CalendarEventStore.
- `src/adapters/` has D1 + memory stores and a fetch-based GoogleCalendarClient.
- `migrations/0001_calendar_google.sql` owns calendar_tokens, calendar_sync_state,
  calendar_channels, calendar_events.
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest.

The five failure modes this module encapsulates (do NOT remove — they are the point):
1. Single-flight OAuth refresh. Concurrent workers must not all refresh at once.
   refreshAccessToken claims an atomic DB lease (TokenStore.acquireRefreshLock);
   one caller calls Google, the rest wait and reuse the stored token.
2. Watch-channel renewal. Google push channels expire (~7 days); renewExpiringChannels
   renews channels in the renewal window (renew-then-stop, no delivery gap) so sync
   does not silently stop after a week.
3. Incremental sync + 410 resync. syncCalendar pages with the stored syncToken; when
   the GoogleCalendarClient signals { gone: true } (HTTP 410) it clears the cursor,
   bumps resyncCount, and full-resyncs.
4. Pure RRULE expansion. expandRecurrence(rules, dtstart, dtend, window) is
   deterministic (UTC instants, no Date.now). Subset: FREQ=DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY,
   INTERVAL, COUNT/UNTIL. Unsupported parts are ignored, not guessed.
5. Push+poll dedup. CalendarEventStore.upsert returns inserted|updated|duplicate by
   (tenant, calendar, googleEventId) + the `updated` marker; the change hook fires
   once. An event seen via push AND poll is processed exactly once.

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral. ALL Google HTTP lives behind GoogleCalendarClient; the only
  file that calls fetch is src/adapters/fetch-google-client.ts.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject the memory adapters and a fake client.
- OAuth client id/secret are injected secrets — never hard-code them.
- Ask before changing migrations, OAuth secret handling, external side effects, or
  production deploy.
